title: Artemis 2 launches on first human mission to the moon in more than 50 years
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission launched on April 1, marking the first human mission beyond Earth orbit in over 50 years. The mission involves four astronauts flying around the moon.

title: SpaceX quietly files for big bang IPO
summary: SpaceX has confidentially filed for an IPO, potentially setting a record-breaking offering. This move is seen as a significant milestone for the space industry.

title: Teledyne forms dedicated space unit to capture rising demand
summary: Teledyne has established a dedicated space unit named Teledyne Space by consolidating its imaging, electronics, and component lines to meet growing demand in the space industry.

title: Artemis 2’s (nearly) 10-day flight around the moon
summary: The Artemis 2 mission will send humans to the vicinity of the moon for the first time in over 50 years, with a flight duration of nearly 10 days.

title: Saltzman: Space ‘baked into’ modern combat operations
summary: Space Force personnel are integrated into modern combat operations, including positions in the Middle East, Tampa, and South Carolina. Gen. Saltzman emphasizes the critical role of space in military operations.

title: Aspect Aerospace secures early funding to advance swarm-deployable VLEO satellites
summary: Aspect Aerospace, a University of South Alabama spin-off, has secured $2.4 million in early funding to develop small, swarm-deployable spacecraft for monitoring the space environment in very low Earth orbit (VLEO). The circuit-board-sized satellites aim to enhance space environment surveillance capabilities.

title: Creating near-term lunar settlements: lessons from space history
summary: The article reflects on the 100th anniversary of Robert Goddard's first liquid-fueled rocket flight and draws lessons from space history to inform near-term lunar settlement efforts. It highlights key milestones like Sputnik and the Apollo 11 moon landing as pivotal moments in space exploration.

title: Artemis 2 fueling underway
summary: Fueling of the Space Launch System (SLS) is underway for the Artemis 2 mission, which aims to send humans to the moon for the first time in over 50 years. The launch is scheduled for April 1.

title: From the Midwest to the Moon
summary: Ohio is emerging as a key hub for space companies, attracting investment and innovation in the industry. The state's strategic advantages are driving growth in aerospace and defense sectors.

title: The Florida Model for Sustainable Aerospace Growth
summary: Space Florida's President and CEO, Robert Long, discusses the Florida Model for sustainable aerospace growth. The conversation highlights strategies to foster long-term development in the aerospace sector.

title: There was a bit of toilet trouble on NASA's Artemis 2 mission to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission to the moon encountered toilet troubles, though it was not the primary issue. The problem highlighted challenges in preparing for the mission.

title: Why is the Artemis 2 rocket launch different from all other rocket launches?
summary: The Artemis 2 rocket launch is notable for being the first crewed mission of NASA's Artemis program, aiming to return humans to the Moon. It differs from other launches due to its specific objectives and the historical significance of the mission.

title: NASA launches 4 astronauts to the moon on historic Artemis 2 voyage, a lunar leap for the 21st century
summary: NASA launched the Artemis 2 mission, sending four astronauts toward the moon for the first time in over 50 years. This marks a historic milestone in lunar exploration for the 21st century.

title: 'Project Hail Mary' end credits showcase stunning nebula photos captured over 400 hours by a single astrophotographer — here's the inside story
summary: The end credits of 'Project Hail Mary' feature nebula photos captured over 400 hours by a single astrophotographer. The project began with an Instagram message and culminated in a cinematic showcase.

title: What do scientists hope to learn from NASA's historic Artemis 2 moon flyby?
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission aims to gather scientific data during its historic moon flyby, with experiments designed to study lunar surface conditions and human health in deep space. The mission will provide insights into the moon's environment and prepare for future crewed lunar landings.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 moon launch may be visible from Florida and southern Georgia today. Here's when to look
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 moon launch may be visible from Florida and southern Georgia today. The event offers a rare opportunity for skywatchers to witness the rocket launch.

title: Watch Artemis 2 fly through space in real time with this telescope livestream
summary: The Virtual Telescope Project will livestream NASA's Artemis 2 Orion spacecraft in real time after its launch. Viewers can watch the spacecraft travel through space via the livestream.

title: April's full 'Pink Moon' rises tonight — just as NASA readies its Artemis 2 moon mission
summary: April's full 'Pink Moon' coincides with NASA's Artemis 2 mission launch, which will send a crew of four to lunar space. The celestial event adds a symbolic backdrop to the historic lunar mission preparations.

title: Satellite spies SpaceX Starlink before it breaks apart | Space photo of the day for April 1, 2026
summary: A satellite captured an image of a SpaceX Starlink spacecraft before it disintegrated in orbit. The event was highlighted in a space photo of the day for April 1, 2026.

title: Réunion Island Lava Reaches the Sea
summary: The February 2026 eruption at Piton de la Fournaise on Réunion Island has lasted longer and produced more lava than recent eruptions. The lava flow has now reached the sea.

title: Liftoff! NASA Launches Astronauts on Historic Artemis Moon Mission
summary: NASA successfully launched the Artemis II mission, marking the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years. The SLS rocket lifted off from Kennedy Space Center, carrying four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft.

title: March of the Harmattan
summary: In March 2026, strong Harmattan winds carried Saharan dust across northwestern Africa and toward the Canary Islands, reducing visibility and prompting alerts.

title: Godspeed, Artemis II!
summary: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir shared a photo of an Artemis program patch aboard the International Space Station, highlighting preparations for the upcoming Artemis II mission to return humans to the Moon.

title: Landsat Reveals Reservoir Changes and Bathymetry
summary: Researchers used Landsat data to study reservoir changes and bathymetry. This fills important gaps in understanding reservoir structure and dynamics.

title: Sendoff for Artemis II Crew
summary: The Artemis II crew, including NASA astronauts Victor Glover, Christina Koch, Reid Wiseman, and CSA astronauts Jeremy Hansen and Jenni Gibbons, posed for a photo before a media event on March 27, 2026. Douglas and Gibbons are serving as backup crew members for the mission.

title: Seeing Blue During Schirmacher’s Summer Melt Season
summary: A network of meltwater lakes and drainage channels has turned the Antarctic ice shelf known for its blue ice areas even bluer during Schirmacher's summer melt season.

title: I Am Artemis: Erik Richards
summary: Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager, discusses his role in supporting NASA's Artemis mission, highlighting his career in spacecraft communication. His journey reflects a lifelong passion for spaceflight inspired by the Space Shuttle Program.

title: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
summary: NASA awarded Intuitive Machines a $180.4 million contract to deliver seven payloads, including five NASA-funded ones, to the Moon under the CLPS initiative and Artemis program. The mission aims to enhance understanding of the lunar surface's chemical composition.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: The Artemis II mission aims to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. This marks a significant milestone in space exploration following the Apollo 17 mission in 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, including SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz rockets, with liftoffs planned from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is involved in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone as the agency prepares for crewed missions beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights the company's role in advancing space exploration.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit from California for the Transporter 16 mission. This marks another successful rideshare mission for the company.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Gateway lunar outpost. The agency outlined its revised strategy for sustainable lunar exploration.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company, based in Ottobrunn, Germany, had planned to proceed with the launch but faced an issue.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, planned for launch in 2028. Administrator Jared Isaacman and other leaders revealed details about the initiative.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on the Starbase launch pads in preparation for the upcoming Block 3 Starships. This activity highlights SpaceX's efforts to increase launch frequency.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the United States will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets. Launches are planned in multiple countries including the U.S., Russia, China, New Zealand, and Norway.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from Baikonur Cosmodrome's repaired pad. The mission follows repairs made after the previous Soyuz MS-28 launch.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned, but no specific details about the space industry news are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years of achievements with a commemorative booklet highlighting 50 hallmark milestones. The publication reflects on five decades of contributions to space exploration and science.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and has 'phoned home'.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), focusing on planned activities and industrial opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and industry stakeholders.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical heart. The visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 software on 30 March 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to improve satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a simpler and cheaper alternative to bulky existing solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun experiments using ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates microgravity conditions for research. This initiative aims to engage young scientists in space-related studies.

title: Artemis II Is On The Way To The Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is en route to the Moon with four astronauts, including the first woman and person of color to head to the Moon, as well as the first non-American astronaut. The crew includes Reid Wiseman, Christina Koch, Victor Glover, and Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: Exclusive: In-Orbit Manufacturing Startup Dispatch Emerges from Stealth
summary: Dispatch, an in-orbit manufacturing startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $500,000 in funding to develop an uncrewed space station for hosting manufacturing infrastructure in orbit.

title: Antaris Raises $28M Series A
summary: Antaris has secured $28 million in a Series A funding round led by WestWave Capital, with additional investment from Lockheed Martin Ventures and other new investors.

title: Top Space Lawmaker on Moon Base, Artemis Plans
summary: A top space lawmaker expressed strong support for establishing a Moon base as a strategic step towards future Mars missions. The lawmaker emphasized that colleagues are rallying behind the Artemis program and lunar exploration as a foundation for deeper space exploration.

title: Voyager and Icarus Team Up on ISS Robotics Demo
summary: Voyager and Icarus are collaborating on a robotics demonstration aboard the International Space Station (ISS), highlighting advancements in space robotics. This partnership marks a significant step in ISS operations, blending real-world science with futuristic technology.

title: Q&A With Lunar Base Manager Carlos Garcia-Galan
summary: Carlos Garcia-Galan transitioned from managing NASA's lunar-orbiting habitat program to overseeing the agency's plan for a rapid lunar surface base. He was interviewed during NASA HQ's Ignition event.

title: Transporter-16 Sends 119 Payloads to Orbit
summary: Transporter-16 successfully launched 119 payloads into orbit. This marks another high-capacity rideshare mission in the space industry.

title: Starcloud Raises $170M Series A at $1.1B Valuation
summary: Starcloud has raised $170 million in a Series A funding round, valuing the company at $1.1 billion. This investment underscores strong investor interest in orbital data center ventures and highlights Starcloud's unique position with flight heritage.

title: Bellatrix Raises $20M For In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace raised $20 million for developing in-space propulsion technology. The company plans to scale production after flight-qualifying its core technologies.

title: Xona Space Systems Raises $170M Series C to Scale Private GPS
summary: Xona Space Systems has raised $170 million in a Series C funding round to expand its private GPS technology, which offers stronger, more accurate, and secure PNT signals in low Earth orbit compared to traditional GPS.

title: NASA Launches Artemis II in Further Step Toward a Moon Return
summary: NASA launched Artemis II, its first crewed mission under the Artemis program, to test the Orion spacecraft’s life support systems with astronauts aboard. This marks a significant step toward NASA's goal of returning humans to the Moon.

title: CanarySat CEO Antonio Abad Outlines the Sovereign, Secure Approach Behind the Magec Constellation
summary: CanarySat, a new satellite operator backed by Arquimea from Spain's Canary Islands, unveiled its Magec constellation, a Ka-band LEO satellite network. CEO Antonio Abad highlighted the constellation's sovereign and secure approach during SATShow Week.

title: Bloomberg, CNBC Report SpaceX Submitted Confidential Filing for IPO
summary: SpaceX has submitted a confidential filing for its initial public offering (IPO) as reported by Bloomberg and CNBC. This allows the company to submit a draft IPO registration to the SEC privately.

title: Measat Appoints New CTO
summary: Measat Global has appointed John Loke as its new Chief Technology Officer to lead the company's digital evolution and regional strategy.

title: Live in the Booth: Arianespace CEO David Cavaillolès Previews Ariane 6 Ramp-Up
summary: Arianespace is progressing with its launch campaign for Amazon's Leo satellites, following its first launch for its largest customer in February. The next mission is scheduled for April, with CEO David Cavaillolès discussing the ramp-up of Ariane 6.

title: Faced With Starlink Competition, Ground Segment Leaders Turn to Orchestration, Specialization
summary: Ground segment providers are adapting to competition from LEO megaconstellations like Starlink and Amazon Leo by focusing on orchestration and specialization. This shift is driven by the need to innovate in technology and services to remain competitive.

title: Delta Taps Amazon Leo for IFC Rollout on 500 Aircraft
summary: Delta Air Lines has partnered with Amazon's Leo satellite service to deploy in-flight connectivity (IFC) on 500 aircraft starting in 2028. This collaboration marks a significant expansion of Delta's connectivity offerings using Amazon's satellite-based technology.

title: Air Force Awards BlackSky $99M to Large Aperture Optical Payload for Space-Based Imaging
summary: The U.S. Air Force awarded BlackSky a $99 million contract to accelerate the design and development of a large aperture optical payload for space-based imaging. The contract was received from the Air Force Research Laboratory in early March.

title: Claussen Departs, ST Engineering iDirect Names Sridhar Kuppanna as CEO
summary: ST Engineering iDirect has appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as CEO, succeeding Don Claussen who led the company for approximately three years. The leadership change marks a transition in the company's executive team.

title: Es’hailSat and EgyptSat Sign VSAT Partnership Deal
summary: Es’hailSat and EgyptSat have signed a multi-year VSAT partnership deal to enhance connectivity services in the Middle East. The agreement aims to improve regional satellite communication capabilities.
